scene1_hook: Imagine a world where MS is caught before it starts. Where mobility isn't a limitation — it's a solved problem. That world isn't science fiction. It's already being built.
scene2_problem: Right now, living with MS means navigating a world that wasn't designed for you. Driving becomes uncertain. Fatigue makes simple tasks costly. Cognitive fog turns ordinary days into battles you fight alone.
scene3_stakes: But what's at stake isn't just convenience — it's independence. The ability to work, to move through a city, to stay connected, to show up for your life. These are the things MS threatens most.
scene4_solution: In the next ten years, AI becomes a universal accessibility layer. Autonomous vehicles remove the driving barrier entirely. Brain-computer interfaces are moving from labs to clinics. Soft exoskeletons will fit like clothing. And remyelination therapies — already in trials — are learning to repair the damage MS causes, not just slow it.
scene5_ask: The tools to stay working, mobile, and connected are improving faster than most diseases progress. The future of MS isn't just longer life. It's fuller life. Learn more at dmpgh.com.

lines: THE FUTURE OF MS AND MOBILITY
what the next 50 years could look like
